client: Right at Home South Orange County
product: In-Home Companion and Senior Care Services
profile_name: Geriatric Primary Care — South Orange County
description: Independent geriatric or senior-focused primary care practices in the South Orange County ZIP territory. The ideal referral partner sees an established panel of older adults, names aging-relevant conditions or services explicitly on their website, and operates outside a hospital or health-system employment structure. Right at Home is a private-pay in-home care agency — the target physician needs a patient population that can afford or elect private-pay home care services and trusts their doctor to make that referral.
scoring_notes: Independence and confirmed geriatric language are both required for Bullseye. ZIP territory is a hard gate — out-of-territory practices are excluded pre-crawl via the structural filter. Internal Medicine and Family Medicine practices must confirm geriatric_signal_confirmed to pass Gate 3; Geriatric Medicine specialty passes automatically. Concierge and house-call signals are the strongest wedge signals — they prove both patient affluence and physician alignment with proactive aging care.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website indicate the practice is independently owned or physician-owned, rather than employed by or operating as part of a hospital, health system, or large medical group?
signals.note: QUALIFIER. Independent ownership means the physician controls referral relationships and can act without a system compliance layer. Must-have for Bullseye tier.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly name geriatric medicine, geriatrics, senior care, elder care, healthy aging, aging-in-place, or any equivalent term as a focus area or service? Look for word-for-word language — specialty claim in the practice name or About page counts.
signals.note: QUALIFIER. Confirms the physician actively works an older-adult population. Must-have for Bullseye. Internal Medicine and Family Medicine practices without this signal are capped at Contender by no_geriatric_signal.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the practice address or service area fall within the South Orange County ZIP codes: 92660, 92661, 92662, 92663, 92861, 92886, 92887? Use only the address shown on the website — do not infer from city name alone.
signals.note: TERRITORY GATE. Only South OC ZIPs are in the Right at Home franchise territory. Confirmed out-of-territory address triggers zip_outside_territory exclusion.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website describe a concierge practice, direct primary care, membership model, retainer, or annual/monthly subscription for medical services — including any published membership fee or enrollment process?
signals.note: STRONG WEDGE. Concierge and direct primary care models prove both patient affluence and physician willingness to coordinate beyond the standard visit — ideal referral culture for private-pay home care.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly list home visits, house calls, or in-home medical care as a service the practice offers?
signals.note: STRONG WEDGE. A physician who already makes house calls understands the in-home care model and is a natural referral partner for Right at Home.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly name Alzheimer's disease or dementia (any type) as a condition treated or a focus area?
signals.note: Confirms the practice works the memory-care population — one of Right at Home's highest-need care categories.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly name Parkinson's disease or parkinsonism as a condition treated or a focus area?
signals.note: Parkinson's patients frequently need in-home support as motor function declines — strong referral alignment.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly name stroke, stroke recovery, or cerebrovascular disease as a condition treated or a service area?
signals.note: Stroke survivors are a high-referral population for in-home companion and ADL support services.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website use the phrase 'aging in place' or a close variant (e.g. 'age in place', 'remain at home', 'independent living at home') as a stated goal or service philosophy?
signals.note: Exact or near-exact phrase required. Signals explicit alignment with the Right at Home mission — physicians who use this language are already pre-selling the in-home care concept to patients.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website use the phrase 'care coordination', 'family caregiver support', 'caregiver resources', or equivalent language describing active coordination with caregivers or outside care providers?
signals.note: Signals the physician operates as a hub in a care network rather than a transactional visit model — higher referral propensity.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly state that the practice accepts Medicare Advantage plans, lists specific Medicare Advantage insurers, or describes itself as in-network with Medicare Advantage?
signals.note: Medicare Advantage acceptance confirms a senior-heavy panel. Right at Home's private-pay services are complementary to MA-covered clinical care.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly list fall prevention, fall risk assessment, or balance evaluation as a service or program offered?
signals.note: Fall prevention programs indicate proactive senior safety focus — directly adjacent to home safety and in-home companion services.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly list medication management, medication review, or polypharmacy management as a service for elderly or complex patients?
signals.note: Medication management for older adults signals clinical complexity and multi-need patients who often require in-home support.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website or any linked Google Business Profile indicate 30 or more Google reviews for this practice?
signals.note: Confidence signal. Volume of reviews indicates an established, active practice with sufficient patient throughput to generate referrals.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website or any linked Google Business Profile show a Google rating of 4.0 stars or higher?
signals.note: Confidence signal. High patient satisfaction correlates with physician relationship quality — favorable referral partner profile.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the practice have an active, functioning website with meaningful content — not a parked domain, placeholder, or single-page listing only?
signals.note: Confidence signal. An active web presence indicates an engaged practice that invests in patient communication.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website link to or reference a Healthgrades profile, or does the website text mention Healthgrades in context of the practice's ratings or reviews?
signals.note: Confidence signal. Healthgrades presence indicates the practice maintains third-party credentialing and patient-facing profiles.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website indicate the physician is employed by, under contract with, or operating as an employed provider of a hospital or hospital system?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. Hospital-employed physicians cannot make independent referral agreements. Confirmed yes immediately excludes the record.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website indicate the practice is part of, affiliated with, or operated by a health system, large integrated medical group, or IDN (e.g. UCI Health, Providence, Kaiser, MemorialCare)?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. Health system affiliation removes independent referral authority. Confirmed yes immediately excludes the record.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly identify the provider as part of Hoag Medical Group, Hoag Physician Partners, or any Hoag-branded network or employed physician group?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. Hoag is the dominant health system in South OC; Hoag-employed physicians operate under a centralized referral compliance structure that blocks independent home care agreements.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website show clear indicators that the practice is permanently closed, no longer accepting patients, or has ceased operations — such as a closure notice, redirects to a different practice, or a domain that has lapsed?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. Closed or inactive practices have no referral value.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is there no functional website for this practice — returning an error, parked domain, or no site at all at the URL provided?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. No web presence means insufficient evidence to qualify the practice for a referral relationship conversation.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the practice address shown on the website fall outside the South Orange County ZIP codes 92660, 92661, 92662, 92663, 92861, 92886, 92887? Use the address on the website — not the input list ZIP.
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. Outside the Right at Home South OC franchise territory. Cannot generate or fulfill referrals for out-of-territory practices.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website indicate the provider works exclusively in a skilled nursing facility, long-term acute care hospital, nursing home, or similar institutional setting with no outpatient or community-based patient panel?
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION. SNF/LTAC-only providers see institutionalized patients who are not candidates for independent in-home care referrals.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is this an Internal Medicine or Family Medicine practice with no mention of geriatrics, senior care, elder care, aging, or any aging-related condition or program anywhere on the website?
signals.note: TIER CAP. Internal Medicine and Family Medicine practices that show no geriatric signal are general practices — possible referral sources but not priority partners. Cap at Contender pending outreach to confirm senior panel composition.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website explicitly indicate the practice accepts only Medi-Cal or serves exclusively low-income or publicly-funded patients, with no mention of Medicare, Medicare Advantage, private insurance, or self-pay?
signals.note: TIER CAP. Medi-Cal-only patient mix is a poor fit for Right at Home's private-pay model. Cap at Contender — the physician may still refer selectively but volume and fit are lower.
